100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On February 24, 1926, a significant diplomatic breakthrough occurred as the League of Nations convened to discuss strategies to bolster global peace amid rising tensions in Europe. In the United States, the House of Representatives passed a bill aimed at federal aid for the construction of new highways, reflecting the nation’s growing focus on infrastructure. Meanwhile, in the realm of science, renowned physicist Albert Einstein celebrated the announcement of his groundbreaking theory of relativity, further solidifying his place in modern physics.
